questions.question: A landing page is built entirely with div elements. What is the strongest reason to refactor it using semantic elements like header, main, nav, and footer?
options: A) Semantic tags automatically add responsive CSS
B) Semantic structure improves accessibility, maintainability, and document meaning
C) It removes the need for JavaScript
D) It guarantees better SEO rankings instantly
questions.correctAnswer: B) Semantic structure improves accessibility, maintainability, and document meaning

questions.question: A form field has placeholder text but no visible label. What is the main issue?
options: A) Placeholders are enough for accessibility
B) The input loses accessible labeling and usability once placeholder text disappears
C) The browser cannot submit the form
questions.correctAnswer: B) The input loses accessible labeling and usability once placeholder text disappears

questions.question: Your component layout needs horizontal alignment of items in a single row with gaps and vertical centering. Which CSS layout tool is the best default starting point?
questions.correctAnswer: B) Flexbox

questions.question: A dashboard requires rows and columns controlled at the same time, with sidebar and content regions. Which CSS layout model is usually the better fit?
questions.correctAnswer: B) CSS Grid

questions.question: Why is mobile-first styling often preferred in frontend projects?
options: A) It prevents use of desktop screens
B) It starts from constrained layouts and scales upward more predictably with progressive enhancement
questions.correctAnswer: B) It starts from constrained layouts and scales upward more predictably with progressive enhancement

questions.question: A button click should open a modal. Which browser API pattern is directly involved at the first interaction step?
options: A) addEventListener handling a click event
questions.correctAnswer: A) addEventListener handling a click event

questions.question: What does fetch() give you before you call response.json() or response.text()?
options: B) A Promise that resolves to a Response object
questions.correctAnswer: B) A Promise that resolves to a Response object

questions.question: A page feels slow after initial load because one large bundle ships every route and feature at once. Which technique directly addresses that?
options: B) Code splitting and lazy loading
questions.correctAnswer: B) Code splitting and lazy loading

questions.question: What is the main benefit of using browser DevTools during frontend debugging?
options: A) It deploys the app automatically
B) It lets you inspect DOM, CSS, network, performance, storage, and runtime errors in one place
C) It writes tests for you
questions.correctAnswer: B) It lets you inspect DOM, CSS, network, performance, storage, and runtime errors in one place

questions.question: Why does a frontend project typically use npm, pnpm, or yarn?
options: A) To replace the browser rendering engine
B) To manage dependencies, scripts, and build/test tooling
D) To create semantic HTML tags
questions.correctAnswer: B) To manage dependencies, scripts, and build/test tooling

questions.question: What problem does Git solve in a frontend team workflow?
options: B) It tracks changes, supports collaboration, and enables safe branching/history
questions.correctAnswer: B) It tracks changes, supports collaboration, and enables safe branching/history

questions.question: Why are reusable components valuable in a frontend codebase?
options: A) They eliminate all state management
B) They reduce duplication and help keep UI and behavior consistent
C) They remove the need for CSS
questions.correctAnswer: B) They reduce duplication and help keep UI and behavior consistent

questions.question: What is client-side routing?
options: B) Navigating between views in a single-page app without full page reloads
questions.correctAnswer: B) Navigating between views in a single-page app without full page reloads

questions.question: Why is TypeScript often adopted in growing frontend projects?
options: B) It adds static typing that improves refactoring safety and developer feedback
D) It eliminates runtime bugs entirely
questions.correctAnswer: B) It adds static typing that improves refactoring safety and developer feedback

questions.question: What kind of problem do automated frontend tests best help prevent?
options: B) Behavior regressions in UI, interactions, and user flows after code changes
questions.correctAnswer: B) Behavior regressions in UI, interactions, and user flows after code changes

questions.question: Why should frontend developers understand XSS at a practical level?
options: A) It is only a backend concern
B) Rendering untrusted content unsafely in the UI can create serious client-side security issues
C) It only affects SQL databases
questions.correctAnswer: B) Rendering untrusted content unsafely in the UI can create serious client-side security issues

questions.question: What do Core Web Vitals primarily measure?
options: A) Database latency and API uptime
B) Real-user page loading, responsiveness, and layout stability experience
questions.correctAnswer: B) Real-user page loading, responsiveness, and layout stability experience

questions.question: A page looks fine visually but cannot be used by keyboard-only users. Which area is weakest?
questions.correctAnswer: B) Accessibility and interaction semantics

questions.question: What is the strongest reason to keep a frontend app modular with clear folders, components, and utilities?
options: A) It makes the first commit smaller
B) It improves maintainability, onboarding, reuse, and long-term scaling of the codebase
C) It removes the need for tests
questions.correctAnswer: B) It improves maintainability, onboarding, reuse, and long-term scaling of the codebase

questions.question: A production-ready frontend is more than screens and styles. Which set best reflects that expectation?
options: A) Colors, icons, and animations only
B) Accessibility, performance, testing, deployment readiness, and maintainable architecture
D) Git branches and README only
questions.correctAnswer: B) Accessibility, performance, testing, deployment readiness, and maintainable architecture
